# Nightly Research Report — 2026-06-18

**Generated by:** Claude Code (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Sources:** Web search, GitHub issues, package manifests

---

## Executive Summary

Three security issues require human attention before the next deployment: Next.js must be patched from 16.2.6 → 16.2.9 (12 CVEs patched including middleware bypass and SSRF), the Redis **server** image must be updated to include the DarkReplica fix (CVE-2026-23631, critical post-auth RCE), and the PostgreSQL server image should be confirmed at 16.12+ for the oidvector disclosure patch (CVE-2026-2003). The backend FastAPI (0.136.1) is already clear of the April critical RCE (patched at 0.115.8+).

On the product front, the market is validating Blockbuilders' NL-wedge bet — competitors (Capitalise.ai, AlgoBulls Copilot) ship NL-first onboarding, and the no-code algo trading market is projected to grow at 15.4% CAGR to $44B by 2030. Issue #618 (NL-wedge A/B harness) is the highest-value `ready-for-agent` item and has solid PostHog/Next.js implementation references available.

---

## Findings for Active Issues

### Issue #618 — NL-wedge onboarding A/B harness (ready-for-agent)

**What it is:** Randomize new users between the wizard (control) and an NL box (treatment) at signup to measure activation lift.

**Market signal:** Capitalise.ai ("describe in plain English"), AlgoBulls Copilot ("describe strategy → AI builds logic"), and LuxAlgo Quant ("prompt → generate → preview → refine") all ship NL-first onboarding in 2026. The pattern is validated — the question is whether Blockbuilders' variant lifts activation.

**Implementation references:**
- PostHog Next.js experiments: `posthog.com/docs/experiments/installation/nextjs` — covers app-router bootstrap, server-side flag evaluation, and client hydration without flicker.
- Pattern: install `posthog-js`, add `NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` to `.env.local`, wrap layout in `PostHogProvider`, call `posthog.getFeatureFlag('nl_wedge_ab')` in the post-signup routing logic.
- The `getExperimentVariant` helper already used by `wjl_retention_ab` (ADR-0010) can be reused; same PostHog client, same consent gate.
- New users split at the `/auth/callback` routing branch (`has_completed_onboarding=false`). No backend change needed if the NL box is frontend-only.

**Blockers to flag:** ADR-0014 must be ratified before implementation. The NL box UI itself may not be built yet — confirm scope with spec.

---

### Issue #620 — ACTIONS #7 Slice 2: PostHog experiment flag, metrics & guardrails (ready-for-human)

**What it is:** Configure the PostHog feature flag for the NL-wedge experiment and define the guardrail metrics before Slice 1 goes live.

**Implementation references:**
- PostHog A/B/n testing guide at `posthog.com/tutorials/abn-testing` covers rollout %, exposure event, guardrail metrics, and MDE settings.
- The `wjl_retention_ab` experiment (ADR-0010) is a working reference — same project, same event naming conventions (`strategy_saved`, `backtest_completed`, `results_viewed`).
- North-star metric: `results_viewed` within session. Guardrails: `backtest_started` rate must not drop, `wizard_skipped` rate as signal sanity check.

---

### Issue #570 — PRD: "What you just learned" retention A/B (ready-for-agent)

**What it is:** `wjl_retention_ab` PostHog experiment showing/hiding the first-run summary card.

**Status per product.md:** Implemented in full (ADR-0010). The `decideWhatYouLearnedCard` logic, `bb.first_run_summary_card_seen` localStorage gate, and the `test`/`control`/`undefined` arm handling are all in place. This issue may be residually open for spec cleanup.

**Action:** Confirm with human whether this is truly open or can be closed.

---

### Issue #555 — PRD: Activation drop-off cohorts (ready-for-agent)

**What it is:** Add `entry_path` + `authoring_mode` + `time_to_activation` properties to PostHog events for cohort analysis.

**Implementation references:**
- PostHog cohorts are built from event properties; adding `entry_path` ("wizard" | "nl_wedge" | "manual") as a property on `strategy_saved` and `backtest_started` is a 2-line change in each event call.
- `time_to_activation` = timestamp diff between `signup_completed` and `results_viewed`; compute client-side from `localStorage` or PostHog person properties.
- No backend change required for frontend-only property enrichment.

---

### Issue #334 — Evaluate PostgreSQL 18 for backtest workloads (risk-high, needs-plan)

**New signal:** PostgreSQL 18.1 was released in June 2026 with 5 security fixes and 65+ bug fixes. The upgrade path from PG 16/17 is documented and stable. CVE-2026-2003 (moderate, oidvector disclosure) is fixed in PG 16.12+, 17.8+, and 18.x. Given the project already tracks PG 18 evaluation (#334), this advisory adds urgency to at least patching the server image to 16.12+ in the near term.

**Recommendation:** This remains risk-high. Research/plan only. Human approval required before any migration work.

---

### Issues #509–511 — Block evaluator / validator / backtest-intake refactors (needs-spec)

No new external signals this cycle. These are internal architectural improvements. Proceeding to spec when capacity allows.

## Security Risks

### CRITICAL — Next.js 16.2.6 → 16.2.9 required

| Detail | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Current version | 16.2.6 |
| Patched version | 16.2.9 LTS (released 2026-06-09) |
| CVEs patched | 12 (May 2026 batch: CVE-2026-23870, CVE-2026-44572 through CVE-2026-44582) |
| Issue types | Middleware bypass, SSRF, cache poisoning, DoS, XSS (React Server Components) |
| Source | [Vercel May 2026 security release](https://vercel.com/changelog/next-js-may-2026-security-release); [Netlify summary](https://www.netlify.com/changelog/2026-05-08-react-nextjs-security-vulnerabilities/) |

**Action:** `npm install next@16.2.9` in `frontend/`, update `package-lock.json`, rebuild Docker image. AGENTS.md hard rule: no deployment without human approval; flag for immediate human review.

---

### HIGH — Redis server DarkReplica (CVE-2026-23631)

| Detail | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Vulnerability | Use-after-free in Lua replication subsystem |
| Impact | Post-auth RCE, full host takeover |
| Affected | All Redis versions with Lua scripting enabled (pre-May 5, 2026 patch) |
| Patched in | Redis 7.2.x, 7.4.x, 8.2.x, 8.4.x, 8.6.x (patched 2026-05-05) |
| Client library | `redis==7.1.1` (Python client) — not directly vulnerable; server image is the risk |
| Source | [GBHackers](https://gbhackers.com/critical-redis-vulnerability/); [ZeroDay.cloud analysis](https://www.zeroday.cloud/blog/redis-cve-2026-23631-dark-replica) |

**Action:** Confirm the Redis Docker image version used in `docker-compose.yml`. If below the patch date, update the image tag to a patched release (7.4.x or 8.x series). The Python `redis` client (7.1.1) does not need updating for this CVE.

---

### MODERATE — PostgreSQL oidvector disclosure (CVE-2026-2003)

| Detail | Value |
|--------|-------|
| CVSS | 4.3 (Moderate) — requires authenticated DB access |
| Impact | Low-privilege DB user can read a few bytes of server memory |
| Affected | PostgreSQL 14–18 prior to 18.2 / 17.8 / 16.12 / 15.16 / 14.21 |
| Patched | 2026-02-12 |
| Source | [PostgreSQL advisory](https://www.postgresql.org/support/security/CVE-2026-2003); [Rapid7](https://www.rapid7.com/db/vulnerabilities/postgres-cve-2026-2003/) |

**Action:** Verify the PostgreSQL image version in `docker-compose.yml`. Update to 16.12+ (or 17.8+ / 18.x if evaluating the PG 18 upgrade). Low urgency but should be resolved in next planned maintenance window.

---

### CLEAR — FastAPI CVE-2026-2978

Project runs FastAPI 0.136.1. The critical RCE (CVSS 9.8) was patched at 0.115.8. No action needed.

## Implementation References

### PostHog Next.js A/B Experiment Pattern (for #618, #620)

```typescript
// app/layout.tsx or auth callback — check flag after signup
import { usePostHog } from 'posthog-js/react'

const posthog = usePostHog()
const variant = posthog.getFeatureFlag('nl_wedge_ab')  // 'control' | 'nl_wedge' | undefined

// Routing:
// 'nl_wedge' → NL box entry
// 'control' | undefined → wizard (existing flow)
```

- Server-side evaluation (for cold-start without flicker): `posthog.com/docs/experiments/installation/nextjs` shows how to bootstrap flags in `generateMetadata` or middleware using `PostHogClient` from `posthog-node`.
- The existing `getExperimentVariant` helper in the `wjl_retention_ab` implementation is the right starting point; clone and rename.

---

### React Flow / XyFlow 12 — CSS import change for Tailwind 4

The project uses `@xyflow/react ^12.10.0` with Tailwind 3.4.19. When (not if) the project upgrades to Tailwind 4, the CSS import location changes:

```css
/* global.css — Tailwind 4 order matters */
@import 'tailwindcss';
@import '@xyflow/react/dist/style.css';  /* must come after tailwindcss import */
```

Under Tailwind 3 the current pattern in `App.tsx` (`import '@xyflow/react/dist/style.css'`) remains correct. No action needed now but document the migration requirement.

- Source: [React Flow Theming docs](https://reactflow.dev/learn/customization/theming); [React Flow What's New 2025-10-28](https://reactflow.dev/whats-new/2025-10-28)

---

### Node-Based Canvas UX — 2026 Benchmarks

From the Lollypop trading app design guide (June 2026) and no-code builder market analysis:

- **NL-first with visual fallback** is the dominant onboarding pattern in 2026. Users type a strategy idea, the platform scaffolds a starting canvas, and the user refines with drag-drop. Blockbuilders' wizard → canvas flow is the closest existing proxy.
- **Progressive disclosure** in block libraries (show 5 essentials, expand to full palette) matches what the highest-rated tools now ship — the Blockbuilders Essentials mode (section 9.3 of product.md) is aligned with this trend.
- **Health/completeness bar** (product.md §4.17, feature-flagged) is cited as a retention-improving UX pattern for visual builders: users who see real-time completeness feedback complete more strategies and return more often.
- **Inline parameter editing** (popover/bottom-sheet, §4.1) mirrors the "action panel" pattern from node-based workflow tools highlighted in the Workflow Builder SDK research.

## Questions for Human Review

1. **Next.js patch urgency**: Is there a deployment window before EOD today to push the 16.2.6 → 16.2.9 patch? This is the most acute security exposure.

2. **Redis image version**: Which Redis image tag is pinned in `docker-compose.yml`? If it pre-dates 2026-05-05 and uses `latest`, the DarkReplica patch may not have been pulled. Need explicit version pin.

3. **Issue #570 closure**: The `wjl_retention_ab` A/B implementation appears complete per product.md (ADR-0010). Should issue #570 be closed, or is there outstanding spec documentation work?

4. **NL box UI readiness**: Issue #618 is `ready-for-agent` but the NL box UI may not exist yet. What does the treatment arm actually render? Is there a Figma/spec for the NL input surface?

5. **PostgreSQL 18 timeline**: Given CVE-2026-2003 and the PostgreSQL 18.1 release, is now the right moment to fast-track the PG 16 → 18 migration evaluation (#334)?
